bio: Hi, I'm Archita! For as long as I can remember, I've been the person friends and family text when they need a restaurant rec — date night, birthday dinner, quick lunch, you name it. This blog is just me finally putting all of that in one place.

I went to UC Berkeley, where I spent four years eating my way through the East Bay — indulging in endless Cheeseboard runs before class, chocolate almond croissants at Fournée Bakery on Saturday mornings, and pizza nights at Rose Pizzeria. I came out the other side with strong opinions and a deep appreciation for the Bay Area food scene.

These days I live in North Beach (yes, I eat a lot of pasta) and work as a consultant in the Financial District. I'm vegetarian, which people always assume makes this harder — it doesn't, it just makes me more creative. There's nothing I love more than finding a place before it blows up, but I'm equally obsessed with the city classics that have been around forever for a reason. The Bay Area is one of the best places in the world to eat, and I take that very seriously.

facts.label: Best meal in SF
facts.note: You'll know when you try it.

facts.label: Favorite food neighborhood
facts.note: An obvious answer that is still correct.
